lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Ah, [player name]. I was wondering where you'd got to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was it you wanted me to do?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Go to Draynor Village and fetch the Wise Old Man to help save the Colony!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Couldn't I just save it myself?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: No! Now go and do what I told you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Lovely day, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Lovely? Oh - er - if you say so. But please excuse me, I really don't have time to chat about the weather.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the rush?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Trolls! They're attacking my fishing Colony!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gosh, I didn't know there were any trolls around here.

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: These are a sort of sea-dwelling troll. They feed on the monkfish that live in this part of the sea, but just recently they've started invading my Colony!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you need any help?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Well yes, I do. I've done some research about this, and I think our best hope is to get hold of an experienced adventurer.

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: In all the legends I've read, brave adventurers have no trouble vanquishing whatever monsters are menacing the helpless settlement.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've been... reading stories about adventurers?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Yes, I've done my research very carefully. I've always made important decisions based on careful analysis of whatever documents are available to me on the topic. For example, I chose this site for my Fishing Colony because

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: records show this part of the sea to be a prime site for catching monkfish. I don't just fumble along like an amateur!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... fair enough, I suppose! Anyway, you think you need an adventurer?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Yes, that's right. A brave adventurer with plenty of experience in this sort of work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm a brave adventurer! Can I try?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: You? I hadn't thought of that... let's have a look at you...

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: No, I'm sorry, but you simply do not have enough experience. I've got a LOT at stake here, and I can't afford to have an amateur getting in the way.

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Hmmm... You're not what I'm looking for, but... [amount] Quest Points is really rather a lot...

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Ok, the Piscatoris Fishing Colony gratefully accepts your offer of help. When the Colony is safe you shall be handsomely rewarded.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh goody, I love rewards! Now, how can I help?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Well, as qualified as you undoubtedly are, I believe we're going to need a truly expert adventurer if the Colony is to stand a chance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Expert adventurer? I'll wager I've had more adventures than you've had pickled gherkins!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Expert adventurer? I'll have you know I'm in the Guild of Legends!

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: If you could just stop blowing your own trumpet for a minute...

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Excellent! Now, in far-away Misthalin there lives one of the greatest adventurers of all time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... this better be good...

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: His name is lost in the mists of time, but you shouldn't have much trouble finding the Wise Old Man of Draynor Village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Him? What in the heck do you want with HIM?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Oh, do you know him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I've met him. Don't you think he's a bit old?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Maybe, but he's still an expert adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! He's mad! You know he smashed his way into a bank and stole a load of stuff?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Ah well, we must forgive him his little foibles.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's just called 'Wise Old Man'?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Well, he presumably has a name, but that's what he's called now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you want me to fetch this Wise Old Man?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: You don't sound convinced. Look, I'll tell you what I've read about him...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Take THAT!

lines.speaker: Ogre Shaman
lines.text: Ssssss!

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: 'Ello matey!

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: Don't ignore your mate!

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: Hey, matey!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I AM NOT YOUR MATE!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Aaah!

lines.speaker: Shaikahan
lines.text: RAAAaaarrgh!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Lie still, and trouble us no more.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Avaunt, foul fiend!.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Oh deary me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... so you want me to fetch this old man who used to be a great adventurer.

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Yes, that's right. And do watch out for the sea trolls!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll leave you to it, then.

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Fine, fine.

sections.title: Talking to the Wise Old Man
Talking to the Wise Old Man again if the player didn't bring the runes before

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You asked me to get some runes.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Okay, hand 'em over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Excellent. I'll see you outside the Colony. Don't forget to arm yourself - it could be dangerous!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'm guessing you haven't got them all, have you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Well, do get a move on! I need 5 Blood runes, 10 Lava runes and 10 Mist runes, otherwise I'll be no use in a fight.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You used to be a famous adventurer, didn't you?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Heh heh! Yes, I suppose you could say that. But that was all quite a long time ago. I haven't been on what you'd call a 'quest' for a while.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does that mean you'd be interested if someone asked for your help?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Oh? What exactly had you in mind?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Herman Caranos of the Piscatoris Fishing Colony thinks he needs you to save his colony from a tribe of attacking sea trolls.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Hmmm... so I haven't been forgotten... and the world once again is calling for my aid...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it's just one man who smells of fish, not exactly the whole world...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: ... yet this could be the opportunity I've been waiting for...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... yawn...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: ... but I'm a lot older than I used to be, do I still have the strength? Should I retire now, or can I do just one more quest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you're not feeling up to it, I'll leave you alone.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Yes, I'll do it! This can be my final adventure, my last great quest. Saving this colony shall be my swan song.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Swan song?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Legend tells us that a swan, before it dies, sings a song of outstanding beauty as a fitting conclusion to its graceful life. This adventure will be my swan song.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're going to sing a song and then snuff it? Maybe I should tell Herman you've flipped...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: No, you ignoramus! I shall bring my adventuring days to a dramatic close by saving this colony!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great. So long as you're not going to sing.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Yes, that's right, I'm not going to sing. Anyway, I shall need some supplies from you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes? What do you want?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'm rather short of runes. If you could fetch me 5 Blood runes, 10 Lava runes and 10 Mist runes, I'll be able to set off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just happen to have those with me!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Gosh, you're very efficient!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sometimes I amaze even myself.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Ok, hand 'em over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Excellent. I'll see you outside the Colony. Don't forget to arm yourself - it could be dangerous!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, 5 Blood runes, 10 Lava runes and 10 Mist runes.

sections.title: Talking to the Wise Old Man again

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Just hop into the tunnel when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]! I must thank you for persuading me to come - I feel quite invigorated in the sea breeze!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh - er - good. Well, Herman must be inside the Colony now, so we should probably go in.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Are you sure you're ready? I can hear creatures moving around in the trees, and we might have to do some fighting soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready to fight.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Excellent. I think this little tunnel will lead us there...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should go and get ready first.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Yes, better safe than sorry! We can go through this little tunnel when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Wait - I think something's coming!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Wait - I think something's coming!

sections.title: Attempting to talk to him during the battle

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: First fight, talk later!

sections.title: When the Wise Old Man runs out of runes

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Blast - I'm out of runes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Got them all!

sections.title: Speaking to the Wise Old Man after defeating the sea trolls

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Well, it all seems to have gone smoothly so far!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, we're both still alive.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Please go and tell Herman I'm here. Someone's sure to know where to find him.

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: Ah, [player name]! Herman told me to expect you. You'll find him in his office by the eastern wall of the Colony.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks I'll go there now.

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: So, [player name]! I did hear from Herr Caranos that you were to our Colony coming. The Director in his office is waiting for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I'll go there now.

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: No way can I send items to bank for you with great big trolls outside! You save Colony, then Arnold open bank for you.

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: I not have spare stuff to trade with you now. Maybe we trade after you save Colony, ja?

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: No way can I let you collect stuffsies with great big trolls outside! You save Colony, then Arnold help you.

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: That thing broken. Arnold fix it when Colony all safe.

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Finally you've arrived! Did you bring the Wise Old Man?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Indeed, I am here!

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Excellent! You have no idea how grateful I am!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Talking of gratitude, I believe you mentioned some kind of reward...

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Reward? Well... we've got to discuss the Colony's defences now. Perhaps you could go and make yourself useful around the Colony? I'm sure my brother, Franklin, could do with a bit of help. Arnold, the storemaster, said he needed an extra pair of hands too. He's in one of the big storage sheds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I help out, then I get a reward?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Yes, yes... now please excuse us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yoohoo to you two!

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: [player name]? Have you helped Franklin and Arnold yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I haven't.

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Please do so!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm on the case, no problems.

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: That's what I like to hear. Off you go...

sections.title: Talking to Wise Old Man again

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Hello, [player name].

sections.title: Attempting to flatten iron bars before lighting the firebox

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not hot enough to work at the moment.

sections.title: Using the tinderbox or logs on the metal press

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The firebox is at the other end of the machine!

sections.title: Using the iron bars on the firebox

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably try putting metal bars into the OTHER end of the machine.

sections.title: Using the logs on the firebox
Talking to Franklin before lighting the firebox

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: How are you getting on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working on it.

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: Good, good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't remember what you wanted me to do.

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: Put some logs into the metal press in my shed, and light them. Then feed 5 iron bars into the press to roll them into sheets. After that, you'll need to hammer them onto the damaged wall.

sections.title: Using the tinderbox on the firebox
Using the tinderbox on the firebox again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's already lit!

sections.title: Using an iron bar on the metal press
Using an iron sheet on the metal press
Using any other item on the metal press or firebox

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not putting that into the press! I don't want to break it!

sections.title: Talking to Franklin after lighting the firebox and before fixing the wall

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: How are you getting on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, the press is working now, but I still have to patch [the broken sections/[Amount] more bits of wall/the last piece of wall].

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: I hope you don't have too much trouble with that press.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Also I think I need a hammer.

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: Hammer? Not a problem - here's mine.

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: I think you'll need to make [[Amount] more/another] iron [sheets/sheet] first.

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: At least you've got enough iron sheets.

sections.title: Using iron sheet on the broken wall
Using hammer on the broken wall

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: YAAAAARRRRGH!

sections.title: Using any other item on the broken wall

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think that will help.

sections.title: Using iron sheet on an already repaired wall

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This bit of wall is fine. I don't need to mess around with it now.

sections.title: Using hammer on an already repaired wall

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: YAAAAARRRRGH!

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: You should be sticking the sheets to the wall, not to me!

sections.title: Talking to Franklin after fixing the wall

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: How are you getting on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've fixed all 5 bits of wall.

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: Wonderful - thank you very much! Now you can turn your full attention to help Arnold...

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: Oh, and if you've got any of those iron sheets left, I'll be happy to buy them off you once the Colony is safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is turning into a lot of work...

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: Wonderful - thank you very much! You should return to my brother, Herman, and tell him you've finished helping me and Arnold.

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: Oh, and if you've got any of those iron sheets left, I'll be happy to buy them off you once the Colony is safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh good. Now maybe Herman will give me that reward he mentioned!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Herman said you wanted a hand with something.

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: Well, yes, I do. Are you any good at smithing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, I'm quite an expert.

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: Ok, we'll see. Now, the trolls managed to smash part of our western wall earlier today. We need 5 sheets of iron to patch it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Won't iron go all rusty with all the salt water around here?

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: Yes, but it'll do for a few weeks, and we haven't the time or materials to do a proper job just now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alrighty. So where do I get sheets of iron?

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: I've got a press in my shred. You can use it to heat up some iron bars and roll them into sheets. You'll need to fill its firebox first - stick some logs in it and light them.

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: When you've got 5 iron sheets, hammer them onto the damage wall.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do I need to heat the bars? I've been cold- working metal all my life, and I've never bothered heating it.

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: How odd! I'd have expected all your products to be horribly brittle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I'm just talented.

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: Anyway, take this tinderbox and get to work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: Anyway, off you go.

sections.title: Attempting to take fish from nearby barrels

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I should maybe catch my own.

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: You bring me fresh monkfish?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I haven't got any.

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: Well, you go to sea at north-west corner of Colony and catch nice fresh monkfish for Arnold, ja?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got this fish...

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: That not monkfish! Go to sea at north-west corner of Colony and catch MONKFISH!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arnold, I've got some monkfish for you.

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: This? This fresh, but it no good to me raw! You go use range in kitchen!

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: Ah, lovely fresh monkfish straight from the sea! Now you bring me [amount] more, ja?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, right.

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: This is superb, [player name]! Now I store these carefully, and you go help little Frankie.

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: This is superb, [player name]! Now I store these carefully, and you go tell Herr Caranos.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh good, maybe he'll reward me now.

sections.title: Talking to Arnold before helping Franklin

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: Your fresh monkfish superb! But now you go help little Frankie, ja?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes.

sections.title: Talking to Arnold after helping Franklin

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: Now you done helping me and Frankie, you go see what Herr Caranos want next.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get to it.

sections.title: Talking to Franklin after helping Arnold

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: Now you've finished helping me and Arnold, you should see if Herman's got anything else for you to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get to it.

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: You come to speak to me, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes... Herman said you needed me to do something?

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: Ah, that is good. I the storemaster am for this Piscatoris Fishing Colony, and our stores are - ahah - not so good. Indeed, our stores contain not a bit of food!

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: This a catastrophe may become, as difficult it may be to fish while the trolls outside are waiting, and our bellies soon shall rumble loudly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Riiiiight...

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: But now you come here to help Arnold! A good and sturdy warrior you must be indeed to have entered our Colony in these times! So you can the fishing do for us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You want me to catch fish?

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: Ja, this is so. But the fish must then be stored, or stinking rotten they become! So the fish you bring must be shiny fresh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get fresh fish?

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: The monkfish in these waters swim, this is why Herr Caranos build his Colony here.

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: You go catch 5 fresh monkfish from sea over in north- west corner. If you bring stinking old monkfish, Arnold know about it, and Arnold not take them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll need a net or something like that.

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: Ja, is not a problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, five fresh monkfish coming up.

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: Big thanks, [player name]. When Colony safe, Herman reward you good.

sections.title: Talking to the Wise Old Man

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Hello, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've finished helping Franklin and Arnold now.

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Or - er - that's good...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does this mean you're going to reward me now?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: There's something else I need you to do first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There always is.

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Well, the Wise Old Man and I have discussed the situation, and he's come up with a plan...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Yes, that's right. The sheer number of trolls means that the best hope for the Colony is to gather our forces and wipe out all the trolls at once.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that sounds like a nice plan. One big fight, no more trolls.

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: There's a slight problem, though.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Yes, with the bit about gathering our forces.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let me guess - you haven't got any forces to gather!

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: I sent most of the colonists away for their own safety when the trolls started attacking the Colony.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: So what you need to do, [player name], is to fetch us an army!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: In the Wizards' Guild in Yanille, Wizard Frumscone always seems to have a limitless supply of zombies and similar creatures. If you can persuade him to lend us an army, we should be able to defeat these trolls.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, so I just need to go see Wizard Frumscone in Yanille?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Yes, please hurry!

sections.title: Talking to the Wise Old Man again

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Hello, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, old man.

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: [player name] - have you brought an army?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm afraid I haven't asked Wizard Frumscone yet.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Do get a move on, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was it you asked me to do?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Go to the Wizards' Guild in Yanille and ask Wizard Frumscone to lend us an army to save the Colony!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I know it was something like that.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Do get a move on, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: Thanks a lot for your help earlier, but I have a lot to do, so please excuse me...

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: It good to see you, [player name], but many things I have to do with lovely fresh monkfish you bring me earlier.

sections.title: Talking to Frumscone after declining originally

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You said you knew a man who could help me save the Piscatoris Fishing Colony.

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: Oh yes. Now, you mustn't mention this to Zavistic or Distentor, but he's a necromancer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Necromancy? I don't want anything to do with that!

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: Do you like my magic Zombies? Feel free to kill them, there's plenty more where these came from!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: [player name]? Have you spoken to Malignius Mortifer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't asked him yet.

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: He'll probably still be wandering around south of Falador whenever you do want to speak to him.

sections.title: Talking to Herman again before talking to Malignius

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: [player name] - have you brought an army?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wizard Frumscone wouldn't help, but he told me where I could find a wizard who might help. I have to look for him just south of Falador.

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Off you go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: By the way, where's the Wise Old Man gone?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: He said he'd be back in a minute. But never mind him - go to Falador and talk to this wizard!

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: Do you like my magic Zombies? Feel free to kill them, there's plenty more where these came from!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I need your help with something...

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: Oho? Very well, young man, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Piscatoris Fishing Colony, somewhere north of the Gnome Stronghold, is under attack by sea trolls, and they've sent me to ask for an army.

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: An army, heh? But I haven't GOT an army!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They said you might lend us some of your zombies.

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: What? This is a combat training facility, not a library! My magic zombies are not for loan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you can't help the Colony?

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: I'm jolly well not lending them an army! If I did that for you, I'd have to do that for everyone, and I'd never get a moment's peace again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what should I tell the Colony? They've got to give up all their work because you won't help?

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: Actually that does seem rather harsh...

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: I have an idea!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: Now, you mustn't breathe a word of this to Zavistic or Disentor upstairs, but I know a man who could help you get an army.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! Is he a Guild wizard too?

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: No, his work is completely different. He's a necromancer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Necromancy? I don't want anything to do with that!

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: He's not going to have you doing any of the actual spellcasting for it. You'll probably just have to collect some weird item from him and take it to the Colony.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know if I still want to do this.

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: If you don't want to continue the quest, just walk away and think no more about it. But I'm sure you won't have to do anything evil or unnatural yourself.

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: So what's it to be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Enough of this - I'm off.

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: Understandable. But if you ever change your mind, just come and ask me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what the necromancer needs me to do.

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: Excellent. His name's Malignius Mortifer. You'll find him somewhere south of Falador.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't I ask a different necromancer? There's one not so far away.

lines.speaker: Wizard Frumscone
lines.text: Hah! Necromancers aren't helpful people. I don't know of many - most guild wizards won't have anything to do with their practices. I think Malignius will help only because he loves showing off his powers.

sections.title: Talking to Malignius again if bones haven't been given yet

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was it you said you'd need to help me save the Colony?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Bones! I need bones, the normal sort you get from humans and small monsters. If you're having trouble, go to the highwaymen just north of here - they've got bones!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, how could I have forgotten?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some bones for you.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Good. Now I need [Amount] more.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Excellent!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now for the quest?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: From the bones you have given me, I shall make bone seeds. These will contain the essence of your army. But you will need some way to carry the seeds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What had you in mind?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: You must go to the Crafting Guild to ask the advice of the master crafters. There are quite a few of them there, and one of them's sure to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I doubt they'll let me in there without a brown apron.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Here, take this one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

sections.title: Talking to Malignius again before speaking to master crafter

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you spare me a brown apron so I can go to the Crafting Guild to ask the master crafters about transporting bone seeds?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Yes, yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to be doing for the Colony?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Is there something wrong with your memory? I told you to go to the Crafting Guild to ask the master crafters for advice on transporting bone seeds.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: So, [player name], your curiosity leads you to speak to me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need help with saving a fishing colony.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Not when you're dressed like that! I don't help Saradominist filth!

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Hahaha! You want MY help? HAHAHA!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... um... The Piscatoris Fishing Colony needs an army to defend them against attacking trolls.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: And of all the people in the world, you came to ask ME? HAHAHA!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... um... pretty please?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Oh, I'll help! I'll give you GOOD help!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... um... thanks, I think...

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: An army, eh? I can raise you a lovely army of the undead skeletal magi! That'd be just right, wouldn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not going to have to do anything... evil, am I?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: No, I'll keep the fun bits for myself. You'll just have to get a few things for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright - what do you need?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Firstly, I need 7 bones, the normal sort you get from people and small monsters.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some here!

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Hah! And I thought only necromancers and jogres carried bones around with them! Very well, hand 'em over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now for the quest?

sections.title: Talking to any other Master Crafter (including the one with a Skillcape)

lines.speaker: Master Crafter
lines.text: Hello, and welcome to the Crafting Guild. Accomplished crafters from all over the land come here to use our top notch workshops.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me anything about transporting bone seeds?

lines.speaker: Master Crafter
lines.text: Bone seeds? What are bone seeds?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A necromancer is going to bind the essence of an undead army into some bone seeds for me if I can get a container to carry them in.

lines.speaker: Master Crafter
lines.text: That's horrible - ask someone else!

sections.title: Talking to the non-descript Master Crafter closest to the door

lines.speaker: Master Crafter
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me anything about transporting bone seeds?

lines.speaker: Master Crafter
lines.text: Woah dude! That's, like, some seriously maximum stuff you're talking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But do you know what I should carry them in?

lines.speaker: Master Crafter
lines.text: Yeah, you just need a pot with a lid. Do you know how to make pot lids?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, a woman called Tassie taught me.

lines.speaker: Master Crafter
lines.text: Tassie Slipcast? She's one groovy lady.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, yes, very nice. So if I get a pot with a lid, I'll be able to carry bone seeds safely in it?

lines.speaker: Master Crafter
lines.text: Yeah, yeah. You need to chill out more, dude.

sections.title: Talking to the Master Crafter near the door again

lines.speaker: Master Crafter
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was it you said I needed for transporting bone seeds?

lines.speaker: Master Crafter
lines.text: A pot with a lid. Nothing more, nothing less.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've spoken to the master crafter about a container for transporting bone seeds.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: You can take that Saradominist attire off before you try speaking to me about your quest again!

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Ah, excellent. HAHAHA! Have you got such a container?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I haven't got one.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Then get one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have it here.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: I have raised you an army and bound them into bone seeds for you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... um... Thanks!

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Don't mention it, it was fun! HAHAHA!

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Now I will seal the seeds into your pot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. So what do I do with them?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Take the pot to the Colony and open it! HAHAHA! Just don't open it before you get there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Suppose I - ahem - need another one later?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Bring me another of these pots with a lid and I will give you more bone seeds.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: HAHAHA!

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: Herman tells me you've been fetching us an army.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something like that!

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: Interesting. I didn't know magic could do that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You learn something new every day.

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: [player name]! I hear you fetch mighty army for us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something like that!

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: You go take them to Herr Caranos quickly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The trolls are dead!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: They weren't very tough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... dear...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Leave this to me...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Aahh, that was fun.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you do that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Talent, my dear.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I... can't... move... [player name], you must fight this for me...

sections.title: Entering the colony without defeating the queen

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Hello, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, old man.

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: [player name] - have you brought an army?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've got it here!

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: You've got what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The army's in this pot!

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Have you been drinking?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, really, the wizard told me he'd put bone seeds in this pot, and when I opened it the army would come forth from the bone seeds.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Frumscone gave you bone seeds?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... um...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Hmmmph. I don't much like the sound of this. I certainly won't be sending anyone to Frumscone for help again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... it wasn't exactly...

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: You can talk about that later! Now we've got an army we can get on with beating the trolls!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Well, I suppose so. [player name], this could be VERY dangerous. Do you feel ready to fight?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't you going to do the fighting?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Yes, of course. But you, my trusty sidekick, are in this adventure with me, and if I'm going forth into battle, you should make sure you're ready too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready. Let's fight!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Good. May this, my final adventure, end well!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to get some stuff together first.

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Come back to us as soon as you're ready.

sections.title: After killing the Sea Troll Queen

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now I've killed that THING, Herman's sure to reward me!

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: You kick big troll queenie pretty hard, ja?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I don't think you'll have any more trouble with the trolls in future.

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: Piscatoris Colony owe you big-time for your help. You go see Hermie - maybe he got something for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Hermie'?

lines.speaker: Arnold Lydspor
lines.text: Herr Herman Caranos. I call him 'Hermie' when him not listening. Sometimes I call him worse things, but not today.

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: I say, you certainly gave that Troll Queen a good thrashing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, no more trouble from her.

lines.speaker: Franklin Caranos
lines.text: I think Herman would like to talk to you in his office. We all owe you so much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll go and see Herman.

lines.speaker: Ramara du Croissant
lines.text: [player name]? Monsieur Caranos wishes to see you in 'is office. Please, go to 'im!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I'll go see him.

lines.speaker: Everyone else
lines.text: [player name]? Mr Caranos said he wanted to see you in his office. I wouldn't keep him waiting if I were you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I'll go see him.

sections.title: Talking to Herman one last time

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've defeated the Sea Troll Queen!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Your fight was truly magnificent, [player name]! Congratulations!

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: [player name], how can I ever thank you? You have saved my Fishing Colony from utter destruction!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, you mentioned something about a reward?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Of course, of course!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I think you've earned a little something from me, too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ooh, goody! Thanks, old man! Um... can I have your hat?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: 'Old man'... yes, I am old. That creature nearly killed me - I have never been so close to defeat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aww, cheer up - we won! But can I have your hat as my reward?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Yes, we won... YOU won... It is definitely time I retired.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So that's the end of your adventuring? Also, how about that hat of yours?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Well, I'm sure I'll find some way to keep myself busy. But yes, this has been my last quest. My swan song.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's been... nice working with you! It'd be even nicer if you'd give me that hat...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: It's been a pleasure working with you too. Do pop in to see me in Draynor soon! I leave you with my blessing...

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Oh, he's gone. I hope he enjoys his retirement.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: HE DIDN'T GIVE ME HIS HAT!

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: It's only a hat!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anyway, you were going to reward me too?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Oh yes, of course! You are hereby welcome to visit the Piscatoris Fishing Colony at any time and make full use of our fishing spots!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is it better to fish here than in Catherby or the Fishing Guild?

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Ah, I'm glad you asked that! You see, this is the only known place where you can catch monkfish!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, so I can catch monkfish. Um... thanks?!

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Yes - there's good eating on a monkfish. They heal more health than the swordfish and lobsters most adventurers carry around, but they're easier to catch than sharks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose that could be useful.

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Indeed! I'm sure Arnold will help you send your monkfish to your bank account. Also, seaweed accumulates in the nets down by the shore, which you might find useful. Franklin says it's much quicker than trying to gather it on Entrana.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe if I ever want to do glassblowing again...

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: Finally, I'd like to give you a little token of my thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you very much!

lines.speaker: Herman Caranos
lines.text: No, thank YOU!
